title: North Texas Night + Food Loop
description: Two polished days across rooftop tacos, heritage retail, brunch, and a vinyl-bar finish sequenced for Atlas demo mode.
weatherForecast: Clear evenings, 74°F highs, and a photo-friendly blue-hour finish.

stops.notes: Anchor the weekend with rooftop tacos while the skyline still reads warm and cinematic.
Use Mule Alley for a slower retail pass and a quick golden-hour photo reset before dinner.
Close the first night once the vinyl room settles into its best amber light and soundtrack.
Brunch by the park keeps day two social without feeling rushed.
Let the atrium light and clean negative space carry the culture portion of the route.

title: Dallas Design Weekend
description: A multi-city design weekend pairing museum light, brunch reservations, gallery calm, and River Walk aperitivo energy.
weatherForecast: Warm afternoons, soft evening cloud cover, and ideal patio weather after sunset.

stops.notes: Kick off with an easy brunch and let the park set a polished pace for the whole route.
Book the atrium slot before afternoon crowds so the gallery still feels quiet and editorial.
Use the vinyl bar as the final Dallas chapter once dinner service winds down.
Mercado breakfast keeps the San Antonio handoff colorful and low friction.
The Pearl gallery hall gives the route a calmer midpoint before evening cocktails.
Arrive twenty minutes before sunset so the canal reflections evolve through blue hour.
Wrap the weekend with a slower art-district stroll and one last wine-bar detour.

title: Hill Country Sunrise Sprint
description: A fast but premium two-day loop with creek overlooks, espresso resets, skyline boardwalk light, wellness calm, and a river launch finish.
weatherForecast: Crisp sunrise air, sunny afternoons, and mild evening temperatures around the lake.

stops.notes: Start early while the creek ledge is still quiet and the canopy keeps the light soft.
Recover with espresso and enough table space to re-sequence the afternoon route if needed.
Hold this one for sunset so the skyline reflections feel like a proper day-one payoff.
Use the bathhouse stop as a recovery chapter before shifting back into active travel.
Finish on the river so the sprint still ends with motion and a clear outdoors-first identity.
